concerns.text: Tasks T027-T029 mandate using 'AVERAGED' predictions across all 15 folds for DeLong's test. This directly contradicts Plan.md (Phase 4) and Constitution Principle VI, which explicitly require 'out-of-fold (OOF)' predictions to maintain statistical validity. Averaging predictions artificially inflates sample size and invalidates the p-value, violating the scientific constraint of the spec.

Task T019 specifies selecting the 'first 20 alphabetically' from rdkit.Chem.Descriptors. The spec (FR-004) requires 'the first 20 non-correlated descriptors'. Selecting alphabetically ignores the 'non-correlated' constraint, risking the inclusion of highly correlated features and violating the requirement for a valid feature set.

Task T022 requires calculating the difference in Recall and reporting if > 5% threshold. The spec (SC-002) states this difference is 'measured against a threshold of 5% to quantify the marginal gain'. The task implies a binary pass/fail or hard threshold enforcement rather than a measurement/reporting step, potentially narrowing the acceptance criterion to a rigid gate not present in the spec.
